meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching and start shipping. Learn how to build a high-converting portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to escape tutorial hell once and for all.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a byproduct of passive consumption. To exit, you must pivot from 'learning' to 'ship-first' development. By utilizing Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform abstract concepts into a tangible portfolio that proves your technical competency to users and investors alike.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the video tutorial ends, and you realize you have built nothing but a folder of abandoned boilerplate code. You are trapped in the cycle of passive consumption, where the illusion of learning replaces the reality of shipping. Founders don't need another 'To-Do List' app; they need a machine that turns ideas into revenue-generating assets.

This guide is your exit strategy. We are abandoning the tutorial loop and moving into the arena of production. By leveraging AI-native workflows, you will bridge the gap between theory and execution, building a portfolio that doesn't just show you can code, but proves you can solve real-world business problems.

steps.title: Define the Value Prop
steps.description: Identify a specific, painful problem in a niche market. Your portfolio project must solve one thing perfectly rather than ten things poorly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate a lean product requirement document for a niche AI-powered productivity tool. Focus on one core feature that saves users 2 hours daily.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a README.md detailing the core value proposition and MVP feature set'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. Focus on a clean, scalable stack that allows for rapid feature iteration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a full-stack Next.js 14 boilerplate with Tailwind CSS, Supabase authentication, and Stripe integration. Keep the structure modular for future scaling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure based on the Antigravity output'

steps.title: Implement the Core Logic with Claude
steps.description: Stop writing boilerplate manually. Use Claude Code to translate your PRD into functional, production-ready code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust service layer in TypeScript that handles the primary user action: processing an AI-based insight from raw user input.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'implement the core service logic in src/services/engine.ts using the provided requirements'

steps.title: Build for Conversion
steps.description: Your UI is your salesperson. Ensure the landing page and onboarding flow are frictionless and value-focused.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a high-converting landing page component using Tailwind CSS that emphasizes social proof and clear call-to-actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'create a landing page component that highlights the core benefit, including a pricing section'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Ship early to get real data. Move from local development to a live URL immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel deployment configuration file that includes environment variable validation for production safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'add deployment scripts and verify production readiness for Vercel'

pro_tips: Treat your code as disposable; prioritize the user feedback loop over architectural perfection during the MVP phase.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your core logic first, then build the UI to match the test requirements.
Document your failures in your portfolio; investors value a founder who can debug a system better than one who never hit a bug.
Integrate analytics (like PostHog) on day one; if you aren't tracking user behavior, you aren't building a business.
Automate your documentation using your commit history; keep the README updated as an automated reflection of your progress.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a custom domain, share the link on IndieHackers or X, and commit to one feature update per week based on actual user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop studying and start building. Every day you spend in tutorial hell is a day your competition captures market share. Stop waiting for the perfect moment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
